arguments.prompt:
Review all MSBuild project files in this .NET solution for anti-patterns, duplication, modernization opportunities, and best practices violations. The solution is at /tmp/sv-5c3827dd5cbd40fbad18852c108b1920/ContosoApp.sln with source in src/ and tests in test/.

Specifically look for:
1. Duplicated properties/settings across project files that should be centralized in Directory.Build.props/targets
2. Package version duplication that could use Central Package Management
3. Anti-patterns (hardcoded paths, restated SDK defaults, Exec instead of built-in tasks, etc.)
4. Modernization opportunities (legacy patterns, unnecessary explicit includes, etc.)
5. Missing or poorly structured build infrastructure (Directory.Build.props, Directory.Build.targets, Directory.Packages.props)

Scan all .csproj, .props, .targets, and .sln files. Produce actionable improvement suggestions with concrete before/after examples where appropriate.

# MSBuild Project File Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Summary

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|
| Projects | 5 (3 src + 2 test) |
| SDK Style | ✅ All projects use SDK-style format |
| TFM | `net8.0` (all) |
| `Directory.Build.props` | ❌ Missing |
| `Directory.Build.targets` | ❌ Missing |
| `Directory.Packages.props` | ❌ Missing |
| `global.json` | ❌ Missing |
| `.editorconfig` | ❌ Missing |

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely to Cause Issues)

### 1. Package Version Conflicts — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced with **different versions** across projects:

| Project | Version |
|---------|---------|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |

This creates a diamond dependency conflict. At runtime, only one version will be loaded. This is exactly the scenario that causes **NU1605** (package downgrade) warnings or subtle runtime bugs.

### 2. Test Framework Version Skew

The two test projects reference **different versions** of the same testing infrastructure:

| Package | `Core.Tests` | `Api.Tests` |
|---------|-------------|------------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

This causes inconsistent test behavior and makes upgrades error-prone.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-Patterns)

### 3. Massive Property Duplication Across All 5 Projects

The following properties are **copy-pasted identically** in every single `.csproj`:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact**: Any change (e.g., upgrading to `net9.0`) requires editing all 5 files. This violates DRY and introduces risk of drift.

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Setting (3 src projects)

All three source projects contain:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

This should be centralized.

### 5. Duplicated Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` (3 src projects)

All three source projects have an identical target:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

This is a maintenance burden and should live in `Directory.Build.targets`.

### 6. No Central Package Management

With 5 projects and 8 distinct packages (some referenced multiple times), package versions are scattered across individual `.csproj` files. This is the #1 cause of version drift (as demonstrated by issues #1 and #2 above).

### 7. Missing `PrivateAssets="all"` on Test Runner Package

In both test projects, `xunit.runner.visualstudio` should have:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="..." PrivateAssets="all" />
```

Without this, the runner DLLs may propagate to downstream consumers if these test assemblies are ever inadvertently referenced.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

### 8. Missing `global.json`

No `global.json` pins the SDK version. This means builds may use different SDK versions on different machines, leading to "works on my machine" issues.

**Add** at solution root:
```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.401",
    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
  }
}
```

### 9. Missing `.editorconfig`

Since `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` is enabled in all projects, there should be an `.editorconfig` defining the rules. Without it, the property has no effect.

### 10. Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` Blocks in Source Projects

Each source `.csproj` has a second `<PropertyGroup>` just for `DocumentationFile`. Consolidate into a single block for readability.

---

## 🟢 Positive (Good Practices)

- ✅ All projects are SDK-style (modern format)
- ✅ `Nullable` and `ImplicitUsings` are consistently enabled
- ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` enforced across the board
- ✅ `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` is set (though needs `.editorconfig`)
- ✅ Test projects correctly set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>`
- ✅ No legacy `packages.config` files
- ✅ No hardcoded absolute paths
- ✅ No unnecessary explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items

---

## Recommended Fix: Introduce Build Infrastructure

### A. Create `Directory.Build.props` (solution root)

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are never packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

### B. Create `Directory.Build.targets` (solution root)

```xml
<Project>

  <!-- Shared informational target for all source projects -->
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>

</Project>
```

### C. Create `Directory.Packages.props` (Central Package Management)

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />

    <!-- Testing -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

### D. Simplified Project Files After Refactoring

**`ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** — from 26 lines to 9:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**`ContosoApp.Services.csproj`** — from 30 lines to 12:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**`ContosoApp.Api.csproj`** — from 32 lines to 14:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Services\ContosoApp.Services.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**`ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** — from 25 lines to 14:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

---

## Summary of Issues by Severity

| Severity | Count | Key Findings |
|----------|-------|--------------|
| 🔴 Error | 2 | Package version conflicts (runtime risk) |
| 🟡 Warning | 5 | Massive duplication, no CPM, missing PrivateAssets |
| 🔵 Suggestion | 3 | Missing global.json, .editorconfig, multiple PropertyGroups |
| 🟢 Positive | 8 | Modern SDK-style, consistent settings intent, no legacy artifacts |

## Priority Action Items

1. **Create `Directory.Packages.props`** — eliminates version conflicts immediately (fixes 🔴 issues)
2. **Create `Directory.Build.props`** — eliminates 7 duplicated properties × 5 files = 35 redundant lines
3. **Create `Directory.Build.targets`** — centralizes the duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target
4. **Slim down `.csproj` files** — remove everything now inherited from shared files
5. **Add `global.json`** — pin SDK version for reproducible builds
6. **Add `.editorconfig`** — make `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` effective

Would you like me to apply these fixes to the project files?

# MSBuild Project File Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Summary

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|

| `.editorconfig` | ❌ Missing |

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely to Cause Issues)

### 1. Package Version Conflicts — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced with **different versions** across projects:

| Project | Version |
|---------|---------|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |

This creates a diamond dependency conflict. At runtime, only one version will be loaded. This is exactly the scenario that causes **NU1605** (package downgrade) warnings or subtle runtime bugs.

### 2. Test Framework Version Skew

The two test projects reference **different versions** of the same testing infrastructure:

| Package | `Core.Tests` | `Api.Tests` |
|---------|-------------|------------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

This causes inconsistent test behavior and makes upgrades error-prone.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-Patterns)

### 3. Massive Property Duplication Across All 5 Projects

The following properties are **copy-pasted identically** in every single `.csproj`:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact**: Any change (e.g., upgrading to `net9.0`) requires editing all 5 files. This violates DRY and introduces risk of drift.

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Setting (3 src projects)

All three source projects contain:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

This should be centralized.

### 5. Duplicated Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` (3 src projects)

All three source projects have an identical target:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

This is a maintenance burden and should live in `Directory.Build.targets`.

### 6. No Central Package Management

With 5 projects and 8 distinct packages (some referenced multiple times), package versions are scattered across individual `.csproj` files. This is the #1 cause of version drift (as demonstrated by issues #1 and #2 above).

### 7. Missing `PrivateAssets="all"` on Test Runner Package

In both test projects, `xunit.runner.visualstudio` should have:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="..." PrivateAssets="all" />
```

Without this, the runner DLLs may propagate to downstream consumers if these test assemblies are ever inadvertently referenced.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

### 8. Missing `global.json`

No `global.json` pins the SDK version. This means builds may use different SDK versions on different machines, leading to "works on my machine" issues.

**Add** at solution root:
```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.401",
    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
  }
}
```

### 9. Missing `.editorconfig`

Since `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` is enabled in all projects, there should be an `.editorconfig` defining the rules. Without it, the property has no effect.

### 10. Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` Blocks in Source Projects

Each source `.csproj` has a second `<PropertyGroup>` just for `DocumentationFile`. Consolidate into a single block for readability.

---

## 🟢 Positive (Good Practices)

- ✅ All projects are SDK-style (modern format)
- ✅ `Nullable` and `ImplicitUsings` are consistently enabled
- ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` enforced across the board
- ✅ `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` is set (though needs `.editorconfig`)
- ✅ Test projects correctly set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>`
- ✅ No legacy `packages.config` files
- ✅ No hardcoded absolute paths
- ✅ No unnecessary explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items

---

## Recommended Fix: Introduce Build Infrastructure

### A. Create `Directory.Build.props` (solution root)

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are never packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

### B. Create `Directory.Build.targets` (solution root)

```xml
<Project>

  <!-- Shared informational target for all source projects -->
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />

</Project>
```
